summary:
The mylohyoid muscle forms the primary anatomic divide separating the sublingual and submandibular spaces, with the sublingual gland located superior to it and the submandibular gland positioned below. This structural relationship confirms its role as the definitive boundary based on consistently reported anatomical placements.  

Note: All provided information supports only one correct answer—Option A—and no conflicting data undermines this point.
